up.method: calculated: this review reports maximum specific activity of S. fumaroxidans in co-culture with M. hungatei of 0.2 mol propionate/g[dw]/day (Scholten and Conrad 2000, cited secondary); converted 0.2 mol/gDW/day = 200 mmol/gDW/day / 24 h = 8.33 mmol/gDW/h

growth_records.cite: Westerholm et al. Syntrophic propionate-oxidizing bacteria in methanogenic systems. FEMS Microbiology Reviews (2022). DOI:10.1093/femsre/fuab057. [PMC8892533]
growth_records.snippet: maximum specific activity for S. fumaroxidans in co-culture with M. hungatei of 0.2 mol propionate/g [dw]/day, more than 10-fold higher than in pure culture with sulfate as electron acceptor
growth_records.notes: Value is a secondary citation within this review article, originally reported by Scholten and Conrad (2000) for chemostat/batch co-culture of S. fumaroxidans with M. hungatei; original paper's own conditions (temperature, pH, dilution rate) are not restated in this review text, so those fields are left null. Rate converted from 0.2 mol propionate/gDW/day to mmol/gDW/h (division by 24). [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature, pH]

growth_records.cite: Plugge CM et al. Metabolic Flexibility of Sulfate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2011.00081. [PMC3119409]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1. Growth rates of selected propionate-degrading bacteria with and without sulfate. Growth rate (day-1) ... Syntrophobacter fumaroxidans | Propionate 0.17 | Propionate + sulfate 0.024 | Van Kuijk and Stams (1995), Harmsen et al. (1998
growth_records.notes: Value compiled in this review's Table 1 (header 'Growth rate (day-1)'); primary source cited = Van Kuijk and Stams (1995); Harmsen et al. (1998). Reported mu = 0.17 day-1 on propionate (syntrophic, no sulfate). Converted mu_h = 0.17/24 = 0.007083 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/0.007083 = 97.9 h (=4.08 d). No medium composition, temperature or pH given in table.

growth_records.cite: Plugge CM et al. Metabolic Flexibility of Sulfate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2011.00081. [PMC3119409]
growth_records.snippet: Syntrophobacter fumaroxidans | Propionate + sulfate 0.024 day-1 | Van Kuijk and Stams (1995), Harmsen et al. (1998)
growth_records.notes: Table 1, propionate + sulfate column. Primary source cited = Van Kuijk and Stams (1995); Harmsen et al. (1998). Reported mu = 0.024 day-1; converted mu_h = 0.024/24 = 0.001 h-1; doubling_time = ln2/0.001 = 693.1 h. No medium/temperature/pH in table.
